mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) and low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a robust labor market, which drives corporate earnings and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when both indicators exceed thresholds.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS > 130000 and UNRATE < 9.7.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signal strong supply-side momentum and demand for construction, which boosts corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO-HOUST combination drops below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) during early-cycle expansion phases indicates robust economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline in 60d horizons when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 217.5 signals potential monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 217.5.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE) reflects weak labor market conditions, which dampen consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.7.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicate strong demand for housing and consumer goods, which boosts economic activity and equity returns, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for HOUST-RSAFS combination drops below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal higher borrowing costs and reduced corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when rates exceed 0.5%.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 0.5%.
